oai_dc:dc.dc:title: In Memoriam ~ Dr. Sam Backos
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Staff, AFA Watchbird
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 51 Number 2 (April/May 2024).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Watchbird 11 1974~2024 In Memory of Daryl “Sam” Backos, DVM Dr. Backos graduated in 1980 from Michigan State University with a degree in Veterinary Medicine. After earning his degree, he worked for the Maryland State lab for six years as a pathologist for the poultry industry. It was during this time that his interest in pet birds heightened and he moved to Ft. Lauderdale, Florida to open an exclusive exotic bird practice in 1986.In the 1980s Sam was known as the avicultural vet because he took the time to visit, help, and learn from some of the most prestigious breeders of that time. He spent long days working with Tom Ireland or Howard Voren during this era, working to solve some of the avian neonatal issues we now take for granted in avian medicine.Sam was a dedicated vet and was always willing to answer calls or even jump in the car and drive somewhere to help a client. Before he opened his clinic in Deerfield Beach, he spent most of his time visiting breeders or helping with avian nursery issues. He was a twenty -four-hour veterinarian and was adored by many in the field. He will surely be missed by many.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 51 No. 2 (2024): Watchbird April/May 2024; 11
